  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The section asserts grid-independent convergence and superiority over the baseline without exposing the reasoning chain; logical consistency of those conclusions could be the primary target of an open-ended 'What do you think?' on a results section.",
    "R1.3": "Claims that the proposed scheme requires 'fewer outer iterations' and 'lower cumulative inner work' than the baseline are stated as facts, but Table 1 is referenced yet not present in the artifact; evidence-claim alignment is materially at stake.",
    "R1.6": "The text contains conspicuous verbatim repetition (experimental setup described twice, conclusions restated twice in consecutive sentences), making prose-level critique a plausible load-bearing interpretation of the open delegation."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Are you asking for a scientific critique (logical consistency of the convergence claims and evidence-claim alignment) rather than writing-quality feedback (prose clarity and repetition)?"
}

rationale.R1.2: The section asserts grid-independent convergence and superiority over the baseline without exposing the reasoning chain; logical consistency of those conclusions could be the primary target of an open-ended 'What do you think?' on a results section.
rationale.R1.3: Claims that the proposed scheme requires 'fewer outer iterations' and 'lower cumulative inner work' than the baseline are stated as facts, but Table 1 is referenced yet not present in the artifact; evidence-claim alignment is materially at stake.
rationale.R1.6: The text contains conspicuous verbatim repetition (experimental setup described twice, conclusions restated twice in consecutive sentences), making prose-level critique a plausible load-bearing interpretation of the open delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for a scientific critique (logical consistency of the convergence claims and evidence-claim alignment) rather than writing-quality feedback (prose clarity and repetition)?
